title: agent_card.v1
description: A signed, verifiable agent capability card: a key attests an identity and a capability set. Carried as the payload of a Treeship receipt with kind=agent_card.v1. A card is 'key-bound' only when its keyid is the envelope signer pinned under AgentCert; self-signed cards are self-asserted. See docs/specs/agent-capability-cards.md.

schema.description: Schema identifier. Must be exactly this value.

agent.description: Actor URI the card claims, e.g. agent://deployer.

keyid.description: Key the card binds to. Meaningful only when it equals the envelope signer; AgentCert-pinned = key-bound, otherwise self-asserted.

owner.description: Optional URI of the human or org that operates the agent.

version.description: Card version (the agent decides the scheme, e.g. semver).

supersedes.description: Content-addressed id of the card this one replaces, or null.

capabilities.description: What the agent claims it can do. Asserted, not proven; checked against evidence by verify-capability. `tools` entries may be exact (file.write) or glob families (file.*).

constraints.description: Self-declared operating constraints, e.g. data_access, requires_human_approval. Asserted.

attestations.description: References to third-party attestations about this card (audit, compliance). Proven only when the referenced receipt is signed by an independent key.

evidence_anchor.description: Optional commitment to the agent's receipt set at mint time (count, latest_receipt_id, merkle_root). Asserted, but commits the agent to a set so post-hoc omission is detectable.

policy_ref.description: Optional policy this card operates under. Narrative reference; Guard consumes it at enforcement time.

capability_provenance.description: Optional per-capability provenance: a map from a capability (same string as in capabilities.tools) to { grade, source?, evidence? }. grade is 'captured' (the tool is wired into the agent's harness, read from config at mint time), 'exercised' (computed by verify-capability from captured receipts), 'discovered' (read from the agent's own published descriptor, e.g. an A2A AgentCard's skills via --from-a2a; a real source, weaker than exercised, stronger than a bare declaration), or 'declared' (an operator's bare assertion). Cards without this block are treated as fully declared. NOTE: a signed grade is only trustworthy when the card is key-bound; verify-capability marks captured/discovered as SELF-ASSERTED for a card that is not key-bound, since a self-asserted card could carry a fabricated grade. See docs/specs/capability-provenance.md.

grade.description: How this capability is known. Constrained to the four defined grades.

source.description: Where the grade came from (a config path, an A2A URL, etc).
